content.problemStatement:
You are given MMM triplets (X1,Y1,B1),(X2,Y2,B2),‚Ä¶,(XM,YM,BM)(X_1, Y_1, B_1), (X_2, Y_2, B_2), \ldots, (X_M, Y_M, B_M)(X1‚Äã,Y1‚Äã,B1‚Äã),(X2‚Äã,Y2‚Äã,B2‚Äã),‚Ä¶,(XM‚Äã,YM‚Äã,BM‚Äã). Find the number of sequences of positive integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã such that for each valid iii, lcm(AXi,AYi)=Bi\mathrm{lcm}(A_{X_i},A_{Y_i}) = B_ilcm(AXi‚Äã‚Äã,AYi‚Äã‚Äã)=Bi‚Äã, or determine that there is an infinite number of such sequences.

Since the answer can be very large, compute it modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007 (109+710^9 + 7109+7). Note that the values B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BMB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_MB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BM‚Äã can also be very large ‚Äï you are given their prime decompositions.
constraints: 1‚â§Xi,Yi‚â§N1 \le X_i, Y_i \le N1‚â§Xi‚Äã,Yi‚Äã‚â§N for each valid iii
prp_rpr‚Äã is a prime for each valid rrr
the total number of different primes prp_rpr‚Äã in each test case does not exceed 555
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (20 points, time limit 2 seconds):** N‚â§20N \le 20N‚â§20
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
MMM lines follow. For each iii (1‚â§i‚â§M1 \le i \le M1‚â§i‚â§M), the iii-th of these lines contains three space-separated integers XiX_iXi‚Äã, YiY_iYi‚Äã and RiR_iRi‚Äã, followed by a space and 2Ri2R_i2Ri‚Äã space-separated integers p1,e1,p2,e2,‚Ä¶,pRi,eRip_1, e_1, p_2, e_2, \ldots, p_{R_i}, e_{R_i}p1‚Äã,e1‚Äã,p2‚Äã,e2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,pRi‚Äã‚Äã,eRi‚Äã‚Äã, denoting that Bi=‚àèr=1RiprerB_i = \prod_{r=1}^{R_i} p_r^{e_r}Bi‚Äã=‚àèr=1Ri‚Äã‚Äãprer‚Äã‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the number of valid sequences modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007, or ‚àí1-1‚àí1 if there are infinitely many valid sequences.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Both elements of AAA can be any positive integers, so there is an infinite number of valid sequences.
Example case 2: There are two constraints, but they are actually identical: lcm(A1,A2)=1\mathrm{lcm}(A_1, A_2) = 1lcm(A1‚Äã,A2‚Äã)=1. The only sequence that satisfies this is A=[1,1]A = [1, 1]A=[1,1].
Example case 3: The two constraints contradict each other, so there is no valid sequence.
